interest: Teachers and their unions (PSZ/PDSZ), plus reform-realist policymakers, seeking to prevent the costs of structural change from being transferred onto an already-overstretched workforce.
value: Feasibility and worker protection versus reform ambition — implementability and fair burden-sharing in tension with the equity goal driving abolition.
fear: That abolition on a fixed short timeline forces redeployment, swells class sizes, and accelerates attrition, degrading STEM/digital capacity and collapsing the reform mid-transition without the promised equity gain.
affected: the governing majority bearing the political/fiscal cost
assumption: That current documented shortage levels cannot be closed by recruitment/retraining within the proposed rollout window, so the timeline itself is the binding constraint unless protections are legislated first.
empirical_uncertainty: Partly settled and partly contested — the shortage is documented (OECD Education at a Glance; PSZ/PDSZ) and the scenario's own implementation_planning concedes a 4-year national capacity build is unlikely, but the exact retraining/redeployment volumes are conceded as unknown, leaving the achievable phase-in pace genuinely uncertain.
